text:
The concept of the "Smart Classroom" has evolved significantly, moving beyond basic digitalization to reflect the sophisticated integration of technology into modern educational spaces. Driven by rapid technological progress and the increasing demand for flexible environments that support both in-person and remote learning, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and emerging smart technologies have become central to the classroom experience. This paper explores the intersection of AI and education through a comprehensive literature review of smart classroom developments.

Our survey categorizes key technologies into three primary pillars: effective classroom management to optimize environmental comfort and safety, the application of smart teaching aids to foster engagement, and the implementation of automated systems for student performance assessment. Beyond reviewing specific technological milestones in these areas, we analyze the underlying role of AI, providing a clear understanding of its necessity in driving these innovations. Furthermore, we employ a SWOT analysis to critically evaluate the strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats associated with adopting AI in educational settings, while addressing future challenges and research directions. This review serves as a roadmap for educators to understand the practical potential and limitations of AI, and for AI professionals to gain insight into the unique pedagogical requirements and technical complexities of educational systems.
